title: Research and Development of Hybrid Pigeonpea
abstract:
The discovery of stable genetic male-sterility in pigeonpea (Cajanus cajan (L.) Millspaugh) and its non- additive
genetic variation for yield has paved the way for breeding hybrids. Male-sterile genes have been transferred
to promising cultivars to develop male-sterile lines. ICPH 8 the first hybrid pigeonpea was released in 1991.
This bulletin describes the advances made in hybrid pigeonpea research and the factors involved in the
commercial exploitation of hybrid seed production.
